examples.title: Unification Algorithm
examples.description: Most General Unifier (MGU) algorithm for FOL
examples.explanation: The unification algorithm finds the most general substitution that makes two terms identical. It handles variables, compound terms, and lists recursively. The occurs check prevents infinite structures.

examples.title: Forward Chaining Algorithm
examples.description: Data-driven reasoning for Horn clause KB
examples.explanation: Forward chaining starts with known facts and iteratively applies rules to derive new facts. It continues until the goal is found or no new facts can be derived.

examples.title: Backward Chaining Algorithm
examples.description: Goal-driven reasoning for Horn clause KB
examples.explanation: Backward chaining recursively tries to prove a goal by finding rules whose conclusions match the goal, then proving each premise. Uses depth-first search.

examples.title: Resolution Refutation
examples.explanation: Resolution refutation proves a goal by adding its negation to the KB and deriving an empty clause (contradiction). Success means the original goal is entailed.
